articles.title: Hexagon Nutrition IPO subscribed 1.65x so far. Here's what GMP hints ahead of second bidding day
articles.summary: The Hexagon Nutrition IPO was fully subscribed within hours, attracting bids for 3.55 crore shares against 2.16 crore available. Retail and NII categories saw high interest, while QIBs did not bid. The IPO runs from June 5-9, 2026, with shares expected to list on June 12.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Reliance, TCS to HDFC Bank: 7 of top-10 most valued firms see market cap erosion by ₹1.25 lakh crore
articles.summary: The market valuation of the seven top firms fell by ₹1.25 lakh crore last week, led by Reliance Industries. The Sensex declined 532.4 points amid persistent FII selling, despite positive factors like lower crude prices and a stronger rupee.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Upcoming IPOs: CMR Green to Hexagon Nutrition - 7 listings, 1 new SME IPO to open this week

articles.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO: Focus shifts to allotment date. Latest GMP, step-by-step guide to check status
articles.summary: CMR Green Technologies IPO allotment is set for 8 June. Investors can check their status via Kfin Technologies. The IPO was oversubscribed 127.04 times. Refunds for unallocated shares start on 9 June, and shares will be credited to demat accounts on the same day, ahead of the listing on 10 June.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: How is the Indian stock market expected to open on Monday after RBI's FII push? Here's what GIFT Nifty signals
articles.summary: Indian equity markets are expected to remain range-bound as domestic policy support meets challenging global cues. The RBI's measures to attract foreign capital boost sentiment, but concerns over crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions keep investors cautious ahead of Monday's trading.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: LIC bets big on Maruti Suzuki, raises stake to above 5% despite weakness in the auto major
articles.summary: Before the transaction, LIC held 1.57 crore shares, representing a 4.989% stake in India's largest passenger vehicle manufacturer. Following the latest acquisition, its holding has risen to 5.006%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: US stock markets this week: S&P 500's 9-week rally ends, falls 2.6%; Nasdaq tumbles 4.5% as tech stocks crash
articles.summary: Wall Street's nine-week rally ended as technology stocks plummeted, with the Nasdaq suffering its largest drop since April 2025. A strong US jobs report heightened fears of prolonged interest rates, contributing to a selloff across major indices, with semiconductor stocks hit hardest.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Crude price fears won't derail economy: PM-EAC's Neelkanth Mishra says India can grow over 8% amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: India's economic growth remains resilient despite rising crude oil prices, as per Neelkanth Mishra of the PM-EAC. He highlights India's strong position to manage oil costs and predicts growth over 8% by early 2026.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s milk output to grow 6% in FY27 despite weak monsoon risks
articles.summary: Higher milk output is expected on the back of an increase in livestock population, better breeding, veterinary care, and expansion of dairy infrastructure through the addition of nearly 75,000 new dairy cooperatives.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt is changing the way you buy cooking oil — standard pack sizes will now help shoppers compare prices
articles.summary: The order targets a common pricing tactic in the world's most populous nation, where oils sold in odd, non-standard sizes leave buyers unable to tell which brand offers the best value for the kitchen staple.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The move into gold by other nations has happened before. It’s bad news for US dollar
articles.summary: The recent shift from U.S. assets to gold mirrors 1971, when central banks dumped dollars before President Nixon ended gold convertibility.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: deficit concerns, IIP reset, India’s wedding dreams
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Solar power curtailment raises red flags for renewable investors
articles.summary: The solar capacity that was curtailed on 1 May equals almost one-sixth of the country’s installed capacity of 150GW. Experts said that low demand due to industries closing for Labour Day on 1 May may have contributed to the curtailment.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India targets near fourfold jump in fisheries exports to $30 billion in 5 years
articles.summary: Commerce minister Piyush Goyal noted that seafood exports rose from around $5 billion in 2013-14 to about $8.5 billion in FY26, while global fisheries trade increased from $150 billion to $164 billion during the same period.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India posts 7.7% GDP growth in FY26, lifted by strong March quarter
articles.summary: Despite the quarterly moderation, the annual figure came in slightly above market expectations for a 7.6% expansion, cushioned by a stronger-than-anticipated January-March performance. The economy had grown 7.1% in 2024-25.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: ‘So what? 100 is just a number’: PM Modi's EAC member dismisses alarm over rupee’s potential fall to triple digits
articles.summary: Shamika Ravi, a member of the PM's Economic Advisory Council, downplays concerns about the Indian rupee reaching 100 against USD, stating it's just a number. She emphasised managing the economic impact of global supply shocks over exchange rate defence.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India GDP Highlights: Q4FY26 GDP at 7.8%, real GDP growth at 7.7%, says govt
articles.summary: India GDP Highlights: MoSPI releases Q4 FY26 and annual GDP data shortly. Track growth rate, sector performance, comparison with estimates, RBI outlook, and market reaction.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI, govt unveil measures to attract foreign inflows in bonds, equities
articles.summary: The RBI and government delivered on a coordinated effort towards shoring up capital flows into Indian assets to ease the pressure on the rupee, experts said
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI MPC outcome: Central bank slashes FY27 growth projections to 6.6% amid Middle East crisis
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in its June policy meeting on Friday lowered its growth projections for the financial year 2026-27 (FY27) to 6.6% from 6.9% earlier amid the Middle East crisis and elevated crude oil prices along with disruptions to the Southwest monsoon.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: War and weather force RBI into a wait-and-watch mode as risks to growth, inflation loom
articles.summary: RBI has trimmed its real GDP growth forecast for FY27 to 6.6% from 6.9% earlier this year, while raising its retail inflation outlook to 5.1% from 4.6%.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil rises as Hezbollah rejects ceasefire, raising fresh fears over West Asia peace
articles.summary: Oil prices rose after Hezbollah rejected a US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, reviving concerns over wider regional tensions and their impact on global energy supplies.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Centre plans to allow PE firms, sovereign wealth funds to directly participate in all greenfield infra projects
articles.summary: The Centre is planning to introduce a new public-private partnership framework to allow PE firms, pension funds, sovereign wealth funds and other large financial investors to directly participate in greenfield project development across sectors like power, railways, airports.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt nears a quarter of ₹80,000-cr asset-sale target for FY27
articles.summary: The government has raised ₹6,366.93 crore through land monetisation via Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs), while stake sales in Central Bank of India and Coal India fetched another ₹7,808.49 crore.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India remains among fastest-growing economies despite global uncertainty: Minister Prasada
articles.summary: The minister highlighted India's push in trade, manufacturing, AI and semiconductors at IIFT's Global Business Research Conference.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: All nine recent FTAs set to be operationalized within 10 months, says Goyal
articles.summary: India expects the nine FTAs signed over the past three years to be operational within 9-10 months. Minister Goyal says these agreements complement India's growth strategy by expanding market access, facilitating trade an industry, enabling movement of talent and attracting investments.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Why has the US proposed fresh tariffs on India?
articles.summary: Experts have argued that India should not fall for the pressure tactics. It should keep the latest Section 301 tariffs and bilateral trade agreements separate.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Crude falls over 1% as Israel-Lebanon ceasefire revives hopes of wider peace
articles.summary: Oil prices fell more than 1% after Israel and Lebanon agreed to renew a ceasefire, easing concerns over supply disruptions and lifting hopes of wider regional peace.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Cabinet clears ₹20,000 cr schemes for jet fuel support, cleaner Delhi vehicles
articles.summary: One-time budgetary support of up to ₹10,000 crore for OMCs to provide ATF price stabilization amid high crude prices. The budgetary support will be in the form of interest-free advances to OMCs.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: How have crude oil prices behaved during the West Asia war?
articles.summary: The West Asia war pushed Brent crude from about $70 to a peak of $126 per barrel, raising fuel costs and inflation in India, which imports 90% of its oil. Prices have eased amid Iran-US peace talks but remain elevated, keeping pressure on growth, consumers and businesses.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India-US trade deal weeks away as negotiators resolve 99% of issues, says US Ambassador Sergio Gor
articles.summary: US Ambassador Sergio Gor said negotiators have resolved 99% of outstanding issues and expect an interim trade agreement within weeks, with only technical matters left to be finalized.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s services sector maintains growth momentum in May as output expands: HSBC India Services PMI
articles.summary: The HSBC India Services Purchasing Managers’ Index, compiled by S&P Global, rose to 59.8 in May from 58.8 in April. It has continued to grow since April, after registering its weakest growth in 14 months in March.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Centre’s domestic financing nearly doubles to ₹3.6 trillion in April, easing capital spending pressure
articles.summary: Strong small savings inflows and residual domestic financing provide a cushion for the government's FY27 spending, reducing its reliance on market borrowings.
articles.label: BULLISH
